name: ETH 4h Range Failure Reacceptance v1
edge_mechanism: a multi-bar breakout loses acceptance, value is reclaimed, and price rotates back through balance instead of continuing
thesis: Cycle 76 explicitly includes range-failure reversal after multi-bar breakout loses acceptance and re-enters balance. ETH 4h is the best-fit lane because it is 100% chop and should reward failed excursions more than continuation. The edge is the reacceptance back into value, not fading random strength blindly.
invalidation_condition: If ETH 4h reacceptance events still undertrade or fail to rotate meaningfully after reclaiming balance, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price spends multiple bars outside a recent range, loses acceptance there, re-enters balance, and rotates back through the range.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Pure chop regimes are dominated by failed excursions rather than durable breaks. Waiting for value reacceptance should provide a structurally grounded reversal instead of blind fading.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the best fit because its regime is pure chop and therefore the most natural home for a range-failure reversal thesis.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: A real breakout can be misread as failed acceptance, causing repeated fades into trend continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries trade but average win size is too small, extend the runner before adding more filters.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Repeated modest balance rotations, many first-target wins, and occasional fuller unwind through the range.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h clears screen density and produces materially better post-cost behavior than recent sparse 4h reclaim families.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated reacceptance entries that immediately re-break the failed side.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because failed multi-bar excursions recur in chop

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit pure-chop lane for range-failure reacceptance reversal.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h shows enough reacceptance rotations with positive first-target expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h remains too sparse or too shallow to monetize.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with many balance rotations.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same failed-break logic survives on a similar major with more transition character.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 4h keeps a tradable version of the same reacceptance mechanism.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h transition pressure breaks the mean-reversion thesis.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Slightly larger but less frequent reversals than ETH.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same idea can gain density on the adjacent timeframe without turning into noise.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 1h increases count while preserving failed-break reacceptance structure.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Higher count, smaller range rotations than 4h.

long: Exit remaining 20% on loss of VWAP or 5 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on reclaim of VWAP or 5 bars
